content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 11 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_011_page_001.jpg

1. Would that you bear with me in a little foolishness! But indeed you do bear with me.

2. For I am jealous for you with a jealousy of God. For I betrothed you to one husband, to present a pure virgin to the Christos.

3. But I fear lest somehow, as the serpent deceived Eua in the craftiness of him, the minds of you may be corrupted from the simplicity and the purity toward the Christos.

4. For if the [one] coming proclaims another Yeshua whom we did not proclaim, or you receive a different spirit which you did not receive, or a different good news which you did not accept, you bear [it] well.

5. For I consider [myself] to have been in nothing less than the super-apostles.

6. And if [I am] unskilled in speech, [I am] not [so] in knowledge, but in every [way] in all [things] having made [it] manifest to you.

7. Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself that you might be exalted, because freely I proclaimed to you the good news of God?

8. Other assemblies I robbed, taking wages for the ministry to you.

9. And being present with you and having been in need, I was not a burden to anyone; for the brothers having come from Makedonia filled up the need of me. And in everything I kept myself unburdensome to you and I will keep [myself so].

10. [The] truth of Christos is in me, that this boasting will not be stopped against me in the regions of Achaia.

11. Why? Because I do not love you? God knows.

12. And what I do, I will also do, that I may cut off the occasion of the [ones] wanting an occasion, that in what they boast they may be found just as also we.

13. For such [ones are] false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into apostles of Christos.

14. And no wonder, for the Satanas himself transforms himself into an angel of light.

15. [It is] not a great [thing] therefore if also the ministers of him transform themselves as ministers of righteousness, whose end will be according to the works of them.

16. Again I say, let no one think me foolish. But if [you do], even as foolish receive me, that I also may boast a little.

17. What I speak, I speak not according to [the] Lord, but as in foolishness, in this confidence of the boasting.

18. Since many boast according to flesh, I also will boast.

19. For gladly you bear with the foolish, being wise [yourselves].

20. For you bear [it] if someone enslaves you, if someone devours [you], if someone takes [from you], if someone exalts himself, if someone strikes you in [the] face.

21. By way of dishonor I say [this], as that we were weak. But in whatever anyone is bold — in foolishness I speak — I also am bold.

22. Are they Hebraioi? So [am] I. Are they Yisraelitai? So [am] I. Are they seed of Abraam? So [am] I.

23. Are they ministers of Christos? — being beside myself I speak — I [am] more: in labors more abundantly, in imprisonments more abundantly, in beatings beyond measure, in deaths often.

24. From Ioudaioi five times I received forty [lashes] less one.

25. Three times I was beaten with rods, once I was stoned, three times I was shipwrecked, a night and a day I have spent in the deep.

26. In journeys often, in dangers of rivers, in dangers of robbers, in dangers from [my own] nation, in dangers from Gentiles, in dangers in [the] city, in dangers in [the] wilderness, in dangers in [the] sea, in dangers among false brothers,

27. in labor and toil, in sleeplessness often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.

28. Apart from the [things] outside, the pressure upon me daily: the care of all the assemblies.

29. Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is caused to stumble, and I do not burn?

30. If it is necessary to boast, I will boast in the [things] of the weakness of me.

31. The God and Father of the Lord Yeshua, the [one] being blessed to the ages, knows that I do not lie.

32. In Damaskos the governor under Aretas the king was guarding the city of [the] Damaskenes to seize me,

33. and through a window in a basket I was let down through the wall and escaped the hands of him.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — "Eua":** Εὔα — Paul's only reference to Eve. The serpent/deception typology parallels the Corinthians' vulnerability to the super-apostles' deception.

2. **v.5 — "super-apostles":** τῶν ὑπερλίαν ἀποστόλων — "the exceedingly great apostles." Whether these are the Jerusalem pillars (Peter, James, John) or Paul's opponents in Corinth is debated. The latter is more likely in context.

3. **v.14 — "angel of light":** ἄγγελον φωτός — Satan disguises himself as a messenger of illumination. This is Paul's most developed statement about Satan's deceptive methods.

4. **v.22 — Triple identity:** Ἑβραῖοι / Ἰσραηλῖται / σπέρμα Ἀβραάμ — three terms for Jewish identity: cultural-linguistic (Hebrew), covenantal-national (Israelite), genealogical-promissory (seed of Abraham).

5. **vv.23-27 — The hardship catalogue:** The most extensive peristasis (suffering list) in Paul. Most of these events are not recorded in Acts.

6. **vv.32-33 — The Damascus escape:** Paul ends his "boasting" with a deliberately anti-climactic escape story — lowered in a basket through a window. This is ironic: instead of a heroic finale, Paul offers a humiliating retreat.

— EMET, AI Translator
